hero.description: We design and develop secure, modern and scalable software that helps dental clinics manage patients, operations and growth with confidence and simplicity.

mission.title: Mission
mission.text: Deliver powerful clinic software that simplifies daily workflows.

vision.title: Vision
vision.text: Become a global leader in dental technology solutions.

team.title: Team
team.text: Skilled engineers focused on real-world healthcare impact.

dentalManagement.title: Dental Management
dentalManagement.desc: Full clinic operations: patients, billing & records.

customSoftware.title: Custom Software
customSoftware.desc: Tailor-made systems built for your workflow.

gettingStarted.welcomeDesc: The Dental Clinic Management System helps you manage patients, record treatments, handle billing, and generate reports — all in one place. Use the sidebar on the left to navigate between sections.
quickStartSteps: Add a patient via Patients → Add New Patient
Record a visit with procedures and clinical notes
View analytics on the Dashboard and Reports pages
Export data as PDF or CSV for your records
gettingStarted.navGuideDesc: Click any card below to jump directly to that section

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.description: Monitor clinic performance with real-time stats, charts, and patient flow tracking.
dashboard.step1: View key metrics: Daily Revenue, Patients Today, Outstanding Balance, and Procedures Performed in the stat cards at the top.
dashboard.step2: Toggle between Daily and Weekly views on the Patients Flow chart to spot visit patterns.
dashboard.step3: Hover over any chart to see detailed tooltips with exact values.
dashboard.step4: Check the Procedure Distribution chart (right side) to see which treatments are most common.
dashboard.step5: Scroll down to the Recent Patients table to see the latest visits and update their status.
dashboard.step6: Data auto-refreshes every 5 minutes — the green pulse indicator confirms live sync.
dashboard.tip1: Click a patient row in the Recent Patients table to jump to their full profile.
dashboard.tip2: Use the daily/weekly toggle to compare today's activity against the weekly trend.
dashboard.tip3: The outstanding balance badge turns red when the amount is high.

patients.title: Patient Management
patients.description: Add, search, and manage patients. Record visits, treatments, and track history.
patients.step1: Navigate to Patients and click 'Add New Patient' to open the registration form.
patients.step2: Fill in patient details (name, phone, age, gender, address) and optionally add medical history (allergies, medications, conditions).
patients.step3: Add procedures directly during registration — select procedure names, specify tooth numbers, and set quantities.
patients.step4: Set a discount and initial payment amount, then the system automatically generates the visit + invoice.
patients.step5: Click on any patient in the list to open their profile with full treatment history, x-rays, and billing info.
patients.step6: Use the search bar to quickly find patients by name or phone number.
patients.tip1: You can add multiple procedures at once during patient registration.
patients.tip2: Tooth numbers are entered as comma-separated values (e.g., 18, 19, 20).
patients.tip3: The patient list shows initials avatars and last visit date at a glance.

visits.title: Visits & Treatments
visits.description: Record new visits, add procedures, and manage treatment records per patient.
visits.step1: From a patient's profile, click 'New Visit' to start a new treatment session.
visits.step2: Select the date (defaults to today) and enter the chief complaint and clinical notes.
visits.step3: Choose procedures from the dropdown — each with quantity and tooth-specific details.
visits.step4: The dental chart visually highlights selected teeth for easy reference.
visits.step5: After saving, the treatment appears in the patient's timeline with status tracking.
visits.step6: Update visit status (Open → Completed → Cancelled) as the treatment progresses.
visits.tip1: Use the dental chart for a visual overview — it's faster than typing tooth numbers.
visits.tip2: Clinical notes are preserved and viewable in the treatment history.
visits.tip3: A single visit can include multiple procedures across different teeth.

billing.title: Billing & Invoices
billing.description: Create invoices, record payments, and track outstanding balances.
billing.step1: Invoices are auto-generated when you create a visit with procedures and a paid amount.
billing.step2: Visit the Billing page to see all invoices with status filters (All, Unpaid, Partial, Paid).
billing.step3: Search invoices by patient name or invoice number using the search bar.
billing.step4: Click any invoice to view details, including itemized procedures, payments, and receipt.
billing.step5: Click 'Record Payment' on an unpaid or partially paid invoice to log a new payment.
billing.step6: Select payment method (Cash, Card, Mobile, Insurance) and add optional notes.
billing.step7: Outstanding balances update automatically — the Billing page shows the total owed at the top.
billing.tip1: Use the status filter tabs to quickly find unpaid invoices needing follow-up.
billing.tip2: The summary bar shows total outstanding, unpaid, partial, and paid counts.
billing.tip3: Each invoice can have multiple payments recorded against it.

reports.title: Reports & Export
reports.description: View analytics and export data in CSV or PDF format.
reports.step1: The Reports page shows summary stat cards for active patients, visits, revenue, and outstanding balance.
reports.step2: The Revenue Trend chart displays monthly revenue over the past year — hover for exact values.
reports.step3: The Visit Distribution bar chart breaks down visits by status (Completed, Cancelled, Active).
reports.step4: To export data, choose your format (PDF or CSV) using the toggle at the top of the Export section.
reports.step5: Click Patient Report, Financial Report, or Treatment Report to download immediately.
reports.step6: PDF exports include professional formatting with headers, dates, and styled tables.
reports.tip1: PDF is best for sharing with colleagues or printing; CSV is best for spreadsheet analysis.
reports.tip2: The revenue chart shows 12 months of data — useful for year-over-year comparisons.
reports.tip3: Exported files are named with the current date for easy organization.

settings.title: Settings & Preferences
settings.description: Configure clinic information, language, and appearance.
settings.step1: Click the gear icon in the top-right header or navigate to Settings.
settings.step2: Update clinic name, phone, and address — this info appears on receipts and exports.
settings.step3: Switch between English and Pashto using the language toggle at the bottom of the sidebar.
settings.step4: Toggle dark/light theme using the sun/moon icon in the header.
settings.step5: All settings are saved automatically and persist across app restarts.
settings.tip1: The clinic name on receipts comes from your Settings — keep it up to date.
settings.tip2: Language and theme preferences are stored in the database.

section.needHelpDesc: Search above or check the FAQ tab for answers to common questions.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.description: Click a question to expand the answer. {{count}} topics covered.
faq.q1: How do I add a new patient with a treatment?
faq.a1: Go to Patients → 'Add New Patient'. Fill in their details, then scroll to 'Procedures' to add treatments right away. Set a discount and payment — the system creates the patient, visit, and invoice in one step.
faq.q2: How do I record a payment on an existing invoice?
faq.a2: Go to Billing, find the invoice (use the search or status filter), click on it to open details, then click 'Record Payment'. Enter the amount, select method, and save.
faq.q3: Can I export treatment data for a single patient?
faq.a3: Yes! Open the patient's profile, scroll to their treatment history, and click the download icon. You can export as CSV (spreadsheet), PDF (formatted report), or plain text summary.
faq.q4: How do I search for a patient?
faq.a4: Press Ctrl+K (Cmd+K on Mac) to open the global search modal from anywhere in the app. Type the patient's name or phone number. You can also use the search bar on the Patients page.
faq.q5: What do the visit statuses mean?
faq.a5: Open = Treatment is in progress. Completed = Treatment is finished. Cancelled = The visit was cancelled. You can change status from the dashboard's Recent Patients table or the patient's profile.
faq.q6: How do I print a receipt?
faq.a6: From the Billing page, open an invoice and scroll to the receipt section. Use your browser's print function (Ctrl+P) or export the invoice details as PDF from the Reports page.
faq.a7: The application stores data locally in a SQLite database. The Reports page does not currently include an automatic backup feature — periodically export your data via the Reports page for safekeeping.
faq.q8: How do I change the language?
faq.a8: Open the sidebar (left panel) and scroll to the bottom. You'll see a language toggle — tap it to switch between English and Pashto. The UI updates immediately.
